records.title: Abortion Legislation Bill
records.abstract: This omnibus bill amends the law to decriminalise abortion, better align the regulation of abortion services with other health services, and modernise the legal framework for abortion currently set out in the Crimes Act 1961 and the Contraception, Sterilisation, and Abortion Act 1977.

records.title: End of Life Choice Bill
records.abstract: This bill gives people with a terminal illness the option of requesting assisted dying.

records.title: Gambling (Gambling Harm Reduction) Amendment Bill
records.abstract: The purpose of this bill is to provide local communities with more power to determine where "pokie" machines may be sited, and in how the proceeds can be distributed.

records.title: Marriage (Definition of Marriage) Amendment Bill
records.abstract: This bill seeks to amend the Marriage Act 1955 to ensure that its provisions are not applied in a discriminatory manner. The bill aims to ensure that all people, regardless of sex, sexual orientation, or gender identity will have the opportunity to marry if they so choose.

records.title: Misuse of Drugs (Medicinal Cannabis and Other Matters) Amendment Bill
records.abstract: The purpose of this bill is to make it legal for New Zealanders who are suffering from terminal illness or any debilitating condition to use cannabis or cannabis products with the support of a registered medical practitioner.

records.title: New Zealand International Convention Centre Bill
records.abstract: The purpose of the Bill is to give effect to an agreement between SkyCity Entertainment Group Limited and the Crown called the New Zealand International Convention Centre Project and Licensing Agreement.

records.title: Sale and Supply of Alcohol (Renewal of Licences) Amendment Bill (No 2)
records.abstract: This bill provides that where a local alcohol policy is in place under the provisions of the Sale and Supply of Alcohol Act 2012 any renewal of a licence under the Act must not be inconsistent with the provisions of that local alcohol policy.

records.title: Shop Trading Hours Act Repeal (Waitaki Easter Trading) Amendment Bill
records.abstract: The purpose of this bill is to allow all retailers within districts covered by the Waitaki Electorate to trade on Good Friday and Easter Sunday.

records.title: Shop Trading Hours Amendment Bill
records.abstract: This bill amends the Shop Trading Hours Act Repeal Act 1990 to grant territorial authorities a limited power to create bylaws that allow shop trading on Easter Sunday, and enable shop workers to refuse work on Easter Sunday.
